prior.content_md:
**MONITORING — EU implementing acts under CountEmissions EU**

Regulation (EU) 2023/1805 mandates that the European Commission adopt delegated acts specifying technical details of the ISO 14083-aligned calculation methodology, including default emission factors and data quality requirements. These delegated acts had not been published in the Official Journal as of the date of this generation run.

- **Issuing body:** European Commission, DG MOVE
- **Expected scope:** Default emission factors by transport mode, minimum data quality tiers, format requirements for emission certificates
- **Why this matters to the workspace:** The delegated acts will determine which default emission factors apply when carrier-specific data is unavailable — a likely scenario for some of the workspace's road subcontractors and charter air operators on specialist verticals (live events, fine art). Until delegated acts are published, there is uncertainty about exactly which fallback values are legally permissible.
- **Research gap:** No official publication date for the delegated acts was confirmed through available sources. The Commission is required to publish before the 1 July 2025 application date; monitoring is required.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/1805 (CountEmissions EU), Official Journal of the European Union, Article 4 (delegated act mandate). https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32023R1805*
